notes: Case A is the real ProductKnowledge from the Searchable Groq run. Features and proof_points are real extractions from the page — no boilerplate. Known issue: the description field still shows boilerplate text ('We help teams...') on some runs — logged in validation_log.md and tracked in CLAUDE.md bug fixes. Case B is a synthetic edge case for the feature-vs-benefit distinction.

expected_output.description: AI search visibility and brand monitoring platform that tracks where brands appear across major AI engines
features: Real-time tracking of brand mentions across AI engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, etc.)
Share of voice measurement across AI search results
benefits: Know exactly where your brand ranks in AI-generated answers
Identify gaps in AI search visibility before competitors do
Quantify share of voice improvement over time
pain_points: Brands have no visibility into how AI engines represent them
Difficulty tracking brand mentions and rankings across multiple AI engines daily
messaging_angles: AI search is the new SEO — and most brands are flying blind

test_cases.validation_note: proof_points[0].text must be the exact string '206% share of voice improvements' — this is the value that flows into StrategyBrief.proof_point. Any paraphrase breaks Contract 1. description field shows boilerplate on some Ollama runs — this is a known issue tracked in CLAUDE.md. The Groq run produces a real description.

test_cases.source: synthetic — edge case for feature/benefit classification

expected_output.description: Project management SaaS for engineering teams integrating with GitHub and Slack
features: GitHub PR status sync to issues
benefits: Eliminates manual status updates across tools
Engineers stay in flow without switching to project management tabs
Sprint retrospectives require no manual data collection

proof_points.text: Reduces standup prep time by 35 minutes per week

pain_points: Manual copy-paste of PR status into project management tickets
Sprint planning meetings where 80% of time is spent on status updates
messaging_angles: The project management tool that updates itself

test_cases.validation_note: This case tests the feature/benefit distinction. 'GitHub PR status sync' is a feature (what the product does). 'Eliminates manual status updates' is the benefit (what the user gains). The agent must not put benefits in the features list or vice versa — a common failure mode. proof_points must have a type field from: stat | customer_count | customer_name | g2_badge | integration_count | uptime_claim.

test_cases.source: synthetic — scrape failure, user document uploaded

input.user_document_content: Product brief PDF with features and positioning

test_cases.validation_note: When scrape fails, the agent must continue with user-provided data only — never crash. proof_points may be empty if the document contains no verifiable stats. The pipeline must not fabricate proof points to fill an empty list. An empty proof_points list is valid output.
